Bus Timings

F32 Bus Timings Mall Of The Emirates to The Sustainable City, Main Entrance and Back

May 29, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

This route is designed to cater to commuters and residents traveling across Al Barsha, Mudon, Arabian Ranches 2, and beyond.

Bus Timings

The F32 bus at the Mall Of The Emirates Bus Station · 4 departs frequently throughout the day. Services start early in the morning and run until late in the evening, ensuring reliable and convenient transportation options for commuters at regular intervals.

Download PDF Bus F32

Bus F32 Timetable

To view the full schedule, you can head over to the official RTA website, or simply follow this link for F32 Bus Timings.

Bus Route

The F32 route includes stops at the following locations:

  1. Mall Of The Emirates Bus Station · 4

  2. Al Barsha A

  3. Lulu Supermarket

  4. Dubai American Academy

  5. Dubai Studio City, Operation Office · 1

  6. Dubai Studio City, Operation Office · 2

  7. Mudon Al Salam Town Centre

  8. Mudon, 2nd Roundabout

  9. Mudon, 3rd Roundabout

  10. Mudon Exit

  11. The Sustainable City, Main Entrance

  12. Arabian Ranches 2, Main Entrance

  13. Dubai Studio City, Operation Office · 1

  14. Dubai Studio City, Operation Office · 2

  15. Dubai American Academy

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Al Barsha A

  2. Mall of the Emirates

  3. Mall of the Emirates 2

  4. Saleh Bin Lahej Building

  5. Mall Of The Emirates Bus Station · 4

Arabian Ranches 2, Main Entrance Bus Stop Intersection

The Arabian Ranches 2, Main Entrance Bus Stop is a central hub where Bus Routes J02 and F32 intersect. This key location provides convenient connections for residents and visitors traveling within the Arabian Ranches area and beyond. The intersection ensures efficient transit options and easy access to various destinations, making it a vital stop for commuters in the region.

Al Barsha A1 Bus Stop Intersection

Al Barsha A1 Bus Stop is a key transit point where Bus Routes F30, J01, F36, F37, and F32 intersect. This station offers convenient connections for passengers traveling within the Al Barsha area and beyond.

Also See: Detailed Information on Dubai Bus X13: Route and Stops

Bus Ticket Price

The fare for the F32 bus route is structured within Dubai’s RTA tariff system, typically ranging between AED 3 to AED 7 depending on the distance traveled. Passengers can pay using a Nol card or exact change when boarding.

Bus Booking

This allows travelers to check schedules, plan routes, and optimize their travel time effectively.

Journey Experience

A trip on the F32 bus route from Mall of the Emirates to The Sustainable City, Main Entrance offers a scenic and diverse journey through Dubai. Starting at the bustling Mall of the Emirates, one of Dubai’s premier shopping destinations, the route passes through several vibrant neighborhoods, giving passengers a glimpse of daily city life.

The long route not only serves commuters but also provides a great opportunity to explore different aspects of Dubai’s modern and sustainable urban development.

Read More: Complete Guide to Abu Dhabi Bus 103: Route and Stops

Traveling with Careem

This service offers a seamless journey across Al Naseem, Al Hebiah 6, and Dubai Land at a fare ranging between AED 53 to 64, providing a flexible and reliable transportation option for commuters and visitors navigating Dubai’s diverse urban landscape.

Whether traveling for work, leisure, or daily errands, the F32 ensures convenient connectivity across the city’s expanding urban landscape.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: May 29, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
